diffCoverage.notes: Reviewed the QR login core across the three declared layers. map_qr_event drops the token-bearing GotLoginInfo; QrLoginEvent carries only non-secret display data; start_qr_login emits only QrLoginEvent and returns AccountProfile; the credential triple is assembled and consumed entirely in-core. zca-rust + reqwest stay confined to the zalo layer.

findings.evidence: The feature's task contract requires ui + smoke + evidence-bundle + review gates, but no UI QR modal exists, the qr_login_live smoke is #[ignore] and has not been run against a real phone scan, and .harness/evidence/qr-login.json is absent.
findings.fix: Build the QR modal (ui step), run `cargo test -- --ignored qr_login_live --nocapture` against a real scan, then write the attested evidence bundle before flipping passes:true.

findings.evidence: The contract requires advisor + security-reviewer + architecture-reviewer. Only this advisor decision exists; the security and architecture reviewer artifacts under .harness/reviews/qr-login/ are not present yet.
findings.fix: Capture security-reviewer.json and architecture-reviewer.json decisions before claiming done.

findings.evidence: ADR-0004 #2 requires a stable per-account imei persisted and reused for re-login. credentials_from_qr generates a fresh imei via generate_zalo_uuid on every call; persistence is deferred to secure-cred-store, so today's imei is not yet stable across runs.
findings.fix: When secure-cred-store lands, persist the generated imei per account in the keychain and reuse it on re-auth instead of regenerating.

findings.evidence: The reqwest Jar is opaque, so cookies_from_jar synthesizes cookie attributes (domain from host, path '/', secure true, expiry None). Only the live smoke can prove the reconstructed triple yields a reusable session.
findings.fix: Confirm reusability in the qr_login_live smoke (login_profile with the issued triple) and capture it in the evidence bundle.

findings.evidence: reqwest was promoted to a direct dependency to read cookies via CookieStore::cookies. ADR-0004 said QR login needs no new dependency beyond a UUID source; reqwest is justified (already in the tree via zca-rust, pinned 0.12, rustls, no native binding) but is a minor letter-vs-spirit note.
findings.fix: Note the direct reqwest dependency in the evidence bundle rationale; no ADR change required.

summary: QR login CORE (types + zalo + command) is correct and safe — no blocking defect. Secret-handling verified: the token-bearing GotLoginInfo event is dropped in map_qr_event (test-asserted), QrLoginEvent models only non-secret display data, start_qr_login emits only QrLoginEvent and returns AccountProfile, and the credential triple is assembled/consumed entirely in-core with value-free errors. Layering holds: types/qr.rs is pure data, zca-rust + reqwest stay confined to the zalo layer, and the change matches scope.allowedLayers [types, zalo, command]. imei is generated via generate_zalo_uuid and the triple is validated. Cookie extraction from the opaque jar is defensive (dedup, graceful skips, empty-cookie rejection). Decision is needs-human (NOT pass) because the feature cannot be honestly marked done yet: the UI modal, the live phone-scan smoke (currently #[ignore], unrun), the evidence bundle, and the security + architecture reviewer artifacts do not exist. feature_list passes:false is correct and must remain until those land.
